#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace std;

struct FenwickTree {
    vector<int> tree;
    int size;
    FenwickTree(int n) : size(n), tree(n + 2, 0) {}
    void update(int idx, int delta) {
        idx += 1; // 1-based index
        while (idx <= size) {
            tree[idx] += delta;
            idx += idx & -idx;
        }
    }
    int query(int idx) {
        idx += 1; // 1-based index
        int res = 0;
        while (idx > 0) {
            res += tree[idx];
            idx -= idx & -idx;
        }
        return res;
    }
    int range_query(int l, int r) {
        return query(r) - query(l - 1);
    }
};

long long compute_depth(long long x) {
    long long depth = 0;
    while (x != 1) {
        x = __builtin_popcountll(x);
        depth++;
    }
    return depth;
}

vector<long long> solve(vector<long long> nums, vector<vector<long long>> queries) {
    int n = nums.size();
    vector<long long> nums_copy = nums;
    // Initialize 5 Fenwick trees for depths 0-4
    vector<FenwickTree> ft(5);
    for (int i = 0; i < 5; ++i) {
        ft[i] = FenwickTree(n);
    }
    // Compute depth for each number and update the Fenwick trees
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        long long d = compute_depth(nums_copy[i]);
        ft[d].update(i, 1);
    }
    vector<long long> answer;
    for (auto& q : queries) {
        if (q[0] == 1) {
            int l = q[1];
            int r = q[2];
            int k = q[3];
            // Check if k is within 0-4, else 0
            if (k > 4) {
                answer.push_back(0);
                continue;
            }
            int res = ft[k].range_query(l, r);
            answer.push_back(res);
        } else {
            int idx = q[1];
            long long val = q[2];
            // Get old depth
            long long old_d = compute_depth(nums_copy[idx]);
            // Remove from old_d's Fenwick tree
            ft[old_d].update(idx, -1);
            // Update nums_copy
            nums_copy[idx] = val;
            // Compute new depth
            long long new_d = compute_depth(val);
            // Add to new_d's Fenwick tree
            ft[new_d].update(idx, 1);
        }
    }
    return answer;
}
